meta.title: Lab Biosafety Assessment — Diagnostic Mycobacteriology Lab, Block-C Room 12
meta.subtitle: Risk-group → biosafety-level determination + containment plan (CDC/NIH BMBL 6th ed / WHO LBM 4th ed)
meta.author: HSE Healthcare
meta.deid_notice: All personnel and specimen-source patients are referred to by role label. The specimen-source patient is referenced by role only; the worker's identity and serological-surveillance / occupational-health record are held confidentially and separately per OSHA 1910.1030(f) / GDPR Art. 9. The lab-incident / exposure summary below is de-identified and aggregated, with categories of fewer than five suppressed. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: Diagnostic mycobacteriology laboratory, Block-C Room 12. Agent: Mycobacterium tuberculosis (primary culture from clinical respiratory specimens). The risk-group → biosafety-level determination (below) was recorded BEFORE any containment or PPE decision. The specimen-source patient identity and the worker's serological-surveillance record were scrubbed to role level before drafting.

The agent's risk group is determined FIRST and is the primary input to the biosafety level. Where an agent's risk group is unknown or unlisted, the assessment emits a literal [GAP] and routes to a competent biosafety officer — it NEVER invents a risk group or a BSL. A BSL assigned from the agent alone, ignoring aerosolization, is FLAGGED.

rows: If unknown → [GAP] + route to a competent biosafety officer (never invent)
sections.caption: The risk-group → BSL determination (BMBL 6th ed / WHO LBM 4th ed) — recorded traceably; an unknown agent risk group is a [GAP], never invented

sections.text: Hierarchy-ranked controls (engineering containment → procedures → PPE last)

Engineering-containment-first: the biosafety cabinet (class matched to the aerosol potential), directional airflow, and waste decontamination are selected BEFORE PPE. A 'wear a respirator and gloves' treatment with no biosafety cabinet or BSL-appropriate facility is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — PPE is the residual barrier matched to the BSL, never the headline control.

rows.control: Class II A2 biosafety cabinet for all aerosol-generating manipulation (primary containment matched to the aerosol potential)
BSL-3 facility — inward directional airflow, sealed room, anteroom; double-door autoclave for waste decontamination
Biosafety procedures + competence sign-off + access restriction; immunisation / serological-surveillance offer
Respiratory protection (fit-tested) + gown + gloves as the residual barrier matched to BSL-3

sections.text: PPE is NOT the primary control. A plan whose headline control is 'wear a respirator and gloves' with no biosafety cabinet / BSL facility, or a BSL selected from a guessed risk group, is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy or a [GAP].

rows: Aerosol-generating manipulation of an RG3 agent — personnel + product + environmental protection
BMBL — decontaminate before disposal; lab-waste stream via hse-india for India
Residual barrier matched to BSL-3 — never the primary control
sections.caption: Primary containment selected by engineering-first hierarchy; PPE is the residual barrier matched to the determined BSL

sections.text: Restricted access and agent accountability where the agent warrants it; the immunisation / serological-surveillance offer is held confidentially. The worker's serological-surveillance / occupational-health record is held confidentially and separately from this assessment (OSHA 1910.1030(f) / GDPR Art. 9). No worker is named in this circulated artifact.

items: Access control — restricted to trained, authorised staff; the agent inventory / accountability maintained per the biosecurity assessment.
Immunisation / serological-surveillance — offered and recorded confidentially; individual results are special-category health data, held separately, never circulated.
Exposure response — immediate first aid → confidential incident report → occupational-health follow-up; the specimen-source patient is referenced by role only.

sections.text: The lab-incident / exposure summary is reported at programme level only; categories of fewer than five individuals are suppressed, with secondary suppression so a suppressed cell cannot be back-calculated from totals. No specimen-source-patient identity, no named worker, and no <5 lab-incident cell is circulated.

rows.title: PPE-led control proposed where engineering containment is required
rows.description: A draft control headlined 'wear a respirator and gloves' (no biosafety cabinet / BSL-3 facility) was flagged and pushed up the hierarchy — the Class II A2 cabinet + BSL-3 facility are the primary containment; PPE is the residual barrier.

rows.title: BSL proposed from the agent alone, ignoring aerosolization
rows.description: An initial BSL was proposed from the agent's risk group without the aerosol-generating procedure; the determination was corrected to include the procedure (the procedure modifies the required containment).

sections.text: After engineering containment (Class II A2 cabinet, BSL-3 facility, autoclave decontamination), the biosafety procedures, and the residual PPE, the residual biosafety risk is framed via risk_matrix as Low–Medium, with the confidential exposure-response pathway as the last line.

rows.action: Verify Class II A2 biosafety-cabinet certification + the BSL-3 directional-airflow log
Confirm autoclave decontamination validation for the lab-waste stream
Run the immunisation / serological-surveillance offer + record uptake confidentially
Review access control + agent accountability against the biosecurity assessment

items: Where an agent's risk group is unknown or unlisted, the determination is a [GAP] routed to a competent biosafety officer — a risk group or BSL is never invented.
Lab-incident / exposure counts taken from the de-identified summary; categories of fewer than five are suppressed — a missing figure is a [GAP], never invented.
India site: state lab-waste / biomedical-waste segregation return owed — [GAP] (resolve the state via hse-india; BMW Rules 2016; no national form number minted).
CDC/NIH BMBL 6th ed (2020) — biosafety levels BSL-1–4 + biosafety-cabinet classes (the containment-level decision)
WHO Laboratory Biosafety Manual 4th ed (2020) — risk groups RG1–RG4 + the risk-assessment approach (the risk-group determination)
NIH Guidelines — recombinant / synthetic nucleic-acid work + IBC oversight (where applicable)
OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1030 — where a lab procedure handles human blood / OPIM ((c) ECP / (d)(2) engineering & work-practice controls / (f) confidential follow-up + HBV vaccination)
UK COSHH 2002 + the ACDP hazard groups (EU/UK binding duty)
India Bio-Medical Waste Management Rules 2016 lab-waste segregation via hse-india (no national form number)
Review trigger: annual / on-agent-change / on-procedure-change / on-incident — whichever is first
Decision-support only — a competent person (a Biological Safety Officer) must review before use.
